This plant belongs to the kingdom Plantae and the phylum Magnoliophyta. intro: Benstonea affinis, also known as Benstonea, is a type of flowering plant in the family Pandanaceae, native to Australia and New Guinea. The Benstonea plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The bioactive constituents of Benstonea affinis include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. Benstonea is often prepared as a herbal tea or tincture, and is used to support immune function, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections.
